policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 runs on 2026-07-16 each passed 970 tests and measured the unchanged 1762-line core scope at 1439 covered lines. The deterministic issue #87 viewshow regression now drives bookmark detail-request retirement through the real lifecycle and covers the existing dom-observer replacement diagnostic; no production core line was added. The reviewed high-water therefore advances by one covered line. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Two clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-16 each passed 1585 tests and measured the identical 26414-line assembly scope at 17837 and 17838 covered lines; the reviewed high-water is 17838. Issue #151 replaces page-local total subtraction with one shared, page-independent upstream-total upper-bound contract for Hidden Content QueryResult, SearchHints, and Seerr JSON shapes. The title issue modal and badge now use a fresh, cache-bypassed, source-pinned mediaInfo.issues owner behind Canopy's issue-view, fresh-binding permission, final configuration-generation, and parental gates; the projection validates the complete relation, treats missing or oversized ownership as explicitly incomplete, filters and sorts before local paging with overflow-safe page math, publishes exact title-local totals, returns up to the bounded 1000-row modal contract, enriches at most six issues concurrently, and never converts malformed or failed reads into a false empty result. Deterministic tests cover a fully blocked first page followed by an allowed second page through the real MVC action filter and response headers, clean and empty pages retaining the incomplete marker, all-blocked/alternating/last-page arithmetic, source-pinned title ownership beyond the former 50-row global limit, exact badge totals, complete 51-row modal retrieval, bounded 75-row enrichment, the 1000-row bound, maximum skip arithmetic, final configuration replacement, permission revocation before fresh dispatch, fresh-detail cache bypass, exact filtering/sorting/paging, blocked and unauthorized targets, malformed/duplicate/missing relations, upstream failures, generic-list compatibility, and a handled prefetch outcome with truthful client failure reporting. The existing two-line tolerance is unchanged and remains the previously reviewed narrow Coverlet instrumentation allowance.
